title: Mask Building Idea
author: Erica Wells
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bd3ji8kn
materials: Glue bottom to front. Match up curved edges.

steps.title: Make your pattern
steps.instruction: Make your pattern. Cut out your first set and use them for tracing. No need for perfection. The felt is around 9 x 12 inches. It's not wildly important that the measurements are exact. It's the shapes really. As long as it fits your face.

steps.title: Glue top to front so that...
steps.instruction: Glue top to front so that it can be tucked under the bottom. Glue along edges.

steps.title: Cut 6 1/2 inches of beading wire
steps.instruction: Cut 6 1/2 inches of beading wire. Use pliers to curl ends.

steps.title: Glue wire into bottom part of mask
steps.instruction: Glue wire into bottom part of mask. Glue to edge.

steps.title: Apply glue to wire curls liberally
steps.instruction: Apply glue to wire curls liberally. Allow time to dry.

steps.title: Cut 2 1/2 inches of flat...
steps.instruction: Cut 2 1/2 inches of flat wire and glue to front. This will be used to adjust over the nose.

steps.title: Cut about 12 to 14 inches of elastic x 2
steps.instruction: Cut about 12 to 14 inches of elastic x 2

steps.title: Tuck inner front into mask
steps.instruction: Tuck inner front into mask. No need for perfection.

steps.title: Fit snuggly to face
steps.instruction: Fit snuggly to face. Use wires to form. Apply like n95. YouTube “n95 mask application” if needed
